_note: Illustrative pilot drug reference (spec §7.4). Replace with a licensed interaction database in production; versioned and updatable out-of-band.

interactions.message: Warfarin + aspirin markedly increases bleeding risk — avoid or monitor INR closely with gastroprotection.
Ciprofloxacin potentiates warfarin — INR can rise sharply; monitor and consider alternative antibiotic.
Possible enhanced anticoagulant effect — monitor INR.
Trimethoprim raises bleeding risk with warfarin — avoid or monitor INR.
NSAID + warfarin — significant GI bleeding risk; avoid.
Triptan + SSRI/SNRI — small risk of serotonin syndrome; counsel and monitor.
Ciprofloxacin sharply raises tizanidine levels — contraindicated.
Corticosteroid + NSAID increases GI ulcer/bleed risk — consider gastroprotection.
Omeprazole may reduce clopidogrel activation — prefer pantoprazole.

ciprofloxacin.note: Avoid with tizanidine; caution with QT-prolonging drugs.

trimethoprim.note: Caution in renal impairment and with warfarin.

oseltamivir.note: Start within 48h of symptoms; adjust for renal function.

enoxaparin.note: Adjust for renal function and weight.
